# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd default authorization policy for a namespace. Configure policy to handle services without explicit authorization rules. Set up default behavior for service-to-service communication. This is for our service mesh policy framework.
# Category: linkerd_policy_default_allow

apiVersion: 'linkerd.io/v1alpha2'  # Required by Linkerd API for applying resource updates in a live environment  
kind: ServiceEntryPolicy 
metadata:
  name: default-authorization     # The policy’s identifier to be used during routing rules creation   
spec:                           
  provider:                      # Specifies the data source that contains resources or an addressable entity for fetching external information like DNS SRV records  
      type: 'k8s.default'        # Required, must match a defined Provider Type in Linkerd API spec file   
statusUpdateSeconds : 60       # The time (in seconds) to poll Kubernetes periodically for changes on the provider status after creation by linkerset apply --recreate policy   This allows us that wait until resources are up and running. It also provides an error condition if there'is a problem with your service-to-service communication setup beforehand
  allowedTrafficPolicy:          # Defines which traffic (routes) should be routed to the destination in response To any given client request, one of these routes will receive it    TLS but not others. These are considered only if 'selector' matches a certain subset clients and those other aren’t
      rulesEmitTimeout: 10s       # Timeout (seconds) for how long Linkerd should wait to emit at least once with the selected subsets   All traffic is allowed unless this timeout occurs, then it assumes some kind of failure occurred in our services.    TLS but not others -> all client requests will be routed only through 'TLS' routes 
      selector:                    # A LabelSelector determines which pods or groups to include when making a service-to-service decision   clients is the label set on any kubernetes Pod, ClientIP matches "10.248.63.9", and Port equals ("default/nginx": 5705)
          labels:                   # A LabelSelector selects which pods to include when making a service-to-service decision   clients is the label set on any kubernetes Pod, ClientIP matches "10.248.63.9", and Port equals ("default/nginx": 5705)
              name: 'client'        # Selects pod with specified labels to send traffic via service-to-service communication (must have those set for selector selectors). The match is based on exact string matches, where both value sets are case sensitive.   Matching label clients in Pod will be selected by the following routes
          ports:                    
            - name : 'default'      # Ports to receive traffic from this service-to-service connection  This field can have multiple values for a single port or when sending all requests you send one of these as default if not set.   If clients are connecting via TLS then they need both the ports: {name:'portName', name2:"otherPort"} and only 'default' will be used in route creation but this is omitted here
              protocol : HTTPS      # The field specifies which network protocols to include when making a service-to-service decision   If clients are connecting via TLS then they need both the ports: {name:'portName', name2:"otherPort"} and only 'default' will be used in route creation but this is omitted here
